raw_data.details: Vidya scheme under the Education Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh is a scheme where a girl child who is studying in Class VI is provided with an incentive of ₹10,000 as fixed deposit in SBI in the name of an individual girl child till she completes Class - XII. After completing Class XII, the Girl child is entitled to receive the maturity amount of the Fixed Deposit. Objective:To encourage girl children to continue school education after elementary and secondary levels. To encourage the girls to increase their presence in schools and reduce their dropout rate at the elementary and secondary stages of schooling. Providing financial assistance/incentive to all female students who successfully pass the Class-V examination and enroll in Class-VI in the State Government Schools. The amount of assistance is ₹10,000 from 2015-16 onwards. The awarded amount will be deposited in the State Bank of India (SBI) in the name of the girl student as Fixed.
raw_data.benefits: Financial assistance: A sum of ₹10,000 (Rupees Ten Thousand) would only be deposited under the bank's Fixed Deposit scheme in the name of every eligible female student. The term/period of deposit may be counted from the date of deposit to the approximate date the girl student passes Class-XII. No premature withdrawal will be allowed. Scheme is beneficial one time on successful completion of Class XII.
raw_data.eligibility: Applicant must be studying in a school in Arunachal Pradesh. Applicant must be an unmarried female Student. Applicant must have a valid class V passing certificate. The mandatory condition for withdrawal is completion of the age of 18 years and class XII. The applicant must have an active saving account with the State Bank of India.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineTo apply for the scheme, the applicant girl students must contact the Head of the school (principal). Collect the application form from the school. Fill the details, attach documents, and submit the application to the school. The concerned authorities may undertake verification of the pre-filled details. Once verified, the school may submit the consolidated list of eligible girl students and details to the Director of Elementary Education. Then the department shall verify and process the applications submitted.
raw_data.documents_required: Class V Pass Certificate from the school concerned, duly countersigned by the DDSE of the district concerned. A recent passport-size photograph of the female student will be pasted on the form. Proof of Passing Class- XII along with verification report of DDSE for withdrawal of maturity amount. Birth CertificateThe active savings account canceled the cheque/passbook for her bank account with SBI.

After completing Class XII, the Girl child is entitled to receive the maturity amount of the Fixed Deposit. Objective:To encourage girl children to continue school education after elementary and secondary levels. To encourage the girls to increase their presence in schools and reduce their dropout rate at the elementary and secondary stages of schooling. Providing financial assistance/incentive to all female students who successfully pass the Class-V examination and enroll in Class-VI in the State Government Schools. The amount of assistance is ₹10,000 from 2015-16 onwards. The awarded amount will be deposited in the State Bank of India (SBI) in the name of the girl student as Fixed. BenefitsFinancial assistance: A sum of ₹10,000 (Rupees Ten Thousand) would only be deposited under the bank's Fixed Deposit scheme in the name of every eligible female student. The term/period of deposit may be counted from the date of deposit to the approximate date the girl student passes Class-XII. No premature withdrawal will be allowed. Scheme is beneficial one time on successful completion of Class XII. EligibilityApplicant must be studying in a school in Arunachal Pradesh. Applicant must be an unmarried female Student. Applicant must have a valid class V passing certificate. The mandatory condition for withdrawal is completion of the age of 18 years and class XII. The applicant must have an active saving account with the State Bank of India. Application ProcessOfflineTo apply for the scheme, the applicant girl students must contact the Head of the school (principal). Collect the application form from the school. Fill the details, attach documents, and submit the application to the school. The concerned authorities may undertake verification of the pre-filled details. Once verified, the school may submit the consolidated list of eligible girl students and details to the Director of Elementary Education. Then the department shall verify and process the applications submitted. Documents RequiredClass V Pass Certificate from the school concerned, duly countersigned by the DDSE of the district concerned. A recent passport-size photograph of the female student will be pasted on the form. Proof of Passing Class- XII along with verification report of DDSE for withdrawal of maturity amount. Birth CertificateThe active savings account canceled the cheque/passbook for her bank account with SBI. Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the Vidya scheme in Arunachal Pradesh? Under the Vidya scheme, girl child who is studying in Class VI is provided with an incentive of ₹10,000 as fixed deposit in SBI in the name of individual girl child till she completes Class - XII. After successful completion of Class XII, the Girl child is entitled to receive maturity mount of Fixed deposit. Who are the Contacts (for clarification/further information)? Concerned Head of the School (Principal) and Deputy Commissioner (DC) and Deputy Director of School Education (DDSE). What are the Financial assistance? The Financial assistance, A sum of ₹10,000 (Rupees Ten Thousand) only would be deposited
under Fixed Deposit scheme of the bank in the name of every eligible girl student. What is The term/period of deposit? The term/period of deposit may be counted from date of deposit to the approximate date on
which the girl student passes Class-XII. What is aim of the scheme? The scheme aims at providing financial assistance/incentive to all girl students who successfully pass Class-V examination and enroll in Class-VI in the State Government Schools. When the beneficiary get the deposited amount? Scheme is beneficial one time on successful completion of Class 12. Is there any premature withdrawal? No premature withdrawal will be allowed. faqs.question: What is the Vidya scheme in Arunachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer: Under the Vidya scheme, girl child who is studying in Class VI is provided with an incentive of ₹10,000 as fixed deposit in SBI in the name of individual girl child till she completes Class - XII. After successful completion of Class XII, the Girl child is entitled to receive maturity mount of Fixed deposit.

faqs.question: Who are the Contacts (for clarification/further information)?
faqs.answer: Concerned Head of the School (Principal) and Deputy Commissioner (DC) and Deputy Director of School Education (DDSE).

faqs.question: What are the Financial assistance?
faqs.answer:
The Financial assistance, A sum of ₹10,000 (Rupees Ten Thousand) only would be deposited
under Fixed Deposit scheme of the bank in the name of every eligible girl student.

faqs.question: What is The term/period of deposit?
faqs.answer:
The term/period of deposit may be counted from date of deposit to the approximate date on
which the girl student passes Class-XII.

faqs.question: What is aim of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims at providing financial assistance/incentive to all girl students who successfully pass Class-V examination and enroll in Class-VI in the State Government Schools.

faqs.question: When the beneficiary get the deposited amount?
faqs.answer: Scheme is beneficial one time on successful completion of Class 12.

faqs.question: Is there any premature withdrawal?
faqs.answer: No premature withdrawal will be allowed.
